title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err0 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err0 on the Hillrom VersaCare scale display when the bed scale system reports a scale control or scale board fault that may affect patient weight accuracy and scale reliability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from patient use if the scale is needed for clinical decisions, medication dosing, fluid monitoring, or daily weights. Do not rely on the displayed weight while Err0 is present. Use another approved method to obtain patient weight if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact error shown on the scale display is Err0. Observe whether it appears during power-up, after plugging into AC power, when attempting to use the scale, or intermittently during normal use.

steps.title: Remove the Bed From Active Scale Use
steps.instructions: Notify clinical staff that the bed scale is not reliable while Err0 is present. Do not document patient weight from the bed scale until the error is cleared and function checks pass.

steps.title: Check External Power First
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is connected to a known-good AC outlet. Inspect the power cord for damage, loose connection, or signs of stress. Confirm the plug is fully seated.

steps.title: Cycle Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power, wait at least 10 seconds, plug the bed back into AC power, and allow the bed electronics to restart normally.

steps.title: Press the Enable Key
steps.instructions: After reconnecting AC power, press the Enable key on the siderail controls and observe whether Err0 clears.

steps.title: Perform Function Checks If Err0 Clears
steps.instructions: If Err0 clears, perform bed function checks, including basic bed movement, siderail control response, scale display response, scale function check per facility process, and visual inspection for damage or loose components.

steps.title: Inspect for Obvious External or Physical Issues
steps.instructions: If Err0 remains, check for damage around the scale display area, liquid intrusion near controls, damaged siderail controls, loose or damaged accessible cables, impact damage, frame damage, or anything interfering with normal bed operation.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Device-Related
steps.instructions: If stable power, power cycling, Enable key use, and visible external checks do not clear Err0, treat the condition as a likely scale electronics fault.

steps.title: Escalate for Scale P.C. Board Repair
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or bench evaluation. Replace the scale P.C. board if qualified and authorized by facility procedure. If replacement does not resolve Err0, contact Hill-Rom Technical Support.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed reported with Err0 on the scale display. Scale function could not be verified for patient weight use.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err0 on scale display. AC power connection checked. Bed power cycled with 10-second wait before reconnecting to AC. Enable key pressed on siderail controls. Error remained, indicating likely internal scale electronics or scale P.C. board fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for repair/bench evaluation. Scale P.C. board replacement recommended per troubleshooting path. If error remains after board replacement, escalate to Hill-Rom Technical Support.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err1 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err1 on the Hillrom VersaCare scale display when the bed scale system fails to operate normally or clear after basic reset steps.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on the bed scale while Err1 is active. Use another approved weighing method if patient weight is clinically needed, and remove the bed from patient use if scale accuracy is required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error
steps.instructions: Confirm the scale display shows Err1 and identify when the error appears, such as during power-up, after plugging into AC power, or when using the scale function.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Basic Bed Power
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is connected to a working hospital-grade AC outlet. Check the power cord, plug, strain relief, and power indicators for obvious issues.

steps.title: Cycle Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power, wait at least 10 seconds, reconnect AC power, allow the bed electronics to restart, and check whether Err1 clears.

steps.title: Enable the Siderail Controls
steps.instructions: Press the Enable key on the siderail controls after restoring AC power, attempt scale operation again, and observe whether Err1 clears or returns.

steps.title: Check for External Scale Interference
steps.instructions: Make sure the bed is not touching walls, carts, furniture, equipment, or another bed. Remove items pulling against the bed frame and inspect the scale control area.

steps.title: Recheck Scale Operation
steps.instructions: With power restored, controls enabled, and external interference removed, attempt normal scale operation. If Err1 clears, perform function checks. If Err1 remains, escalate for repair.

steps.title: Escalate if Err1 Remains
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from service and label it Out of Service. Persistent Err1 after external checks points toward a scale-system repair need, such as scale P.C. board replacement or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed reported with Err1 on scale display. Scale function unavailable during attempted use.
CCR.Cause: Verified Err1 on scale display. Checked AC power, cycled bed power with 10-second wait, pressed siderail Enable key, and inspected for external scale interference. Error cleared after reset, indicating temporary scale electronics fault.
CCR.Resolution: Cycled bed power, enabled siderail controls, confirmed Err1 cleared, performed bed function checks, verified scale display operation, and returned bed to service.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err2 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err2 on the Hillrom VersaCare scale display when the bed scale cannot function normally, may require recalibration, or cannot be trusted for accurate patient weight measurement.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from active patient weighing use if accurate patient weight is required. Use another approved scale or replacement bed if patient weight is needed for medication dosing, fluid management, or clinical documentation. Do not troubleshoot around an active patient if frame movement, cable inspection, or scale system checks are required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the scale display shows Err2. Check whether the error appears during power-up, after the bed is plugged into AC power, when pressing scale controls, after moving or cleaning the bed, or after a patient transfer or bed impact. Press the Enable key on the siderail controls and observe whether the scale display responds normally.

steps.title: Cycle Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power, wait at least 10 seconds, plug the bed back into AC power, press the Enable key on the siderail controls, and check whether Err2 clears from the scale display. If Err2 clears, perform scale function checks and applicable bed function checks before returning the bed to service.

steps.title: Check Power and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is connected to a working hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord and plug for damage. Verify the bed is not operating only on battery power. Check for signs of liquid intrusion, impact damage, or loose external components near the scale display or siderail controls.

steps.title: Attempt Scale Calibration if Err2 Remains
steps.instructions: If Err2 remains after power cycling, follow the approved VersaCare scale calibration procedure according to facility procedure and service documentation. Make sure the bed is properly positioned and unloaded as required before calibration. Avoid touching, leaning on, or loading the bed during calibration.

steps.title: Inspect for Visible Frame or Scale System Problems
steps.instructions: Inspect for damaged frame components, bent weigh frame or non-weigh frame areas, pinched or damaged cables, foreign objects or debris between moving frame sections, interference between the weigh frame and non-weigh frame, and signs of impact from transport, cleaning, or patient transfer.

steps.title: Correct Any Visible Problems Found
steps.instructions: Remove debris or objects causing frame interference. Free any externally pinched cable if it can be done safely and within facility practice. Correct obvious obstruction between weigh and non-weigh frame sections. Do not force bent frame components back into place if structural damage is suspected.

steps.title: Escalate for Repair Evaluation
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from service if no visible issue is found or if correcting visible issues does not clear Err2. Follow approved repair procedure for scale P.C. board replacement if within your facility’s scope. If the error remains after scale P.C. board replacement, contact Hill-Rom Technical Support.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed reported Err2 on the scale display. Scale function could not be verified for patient weight use.
CCR.Cause: Err2 remained after initial scale display verification. Power cycle, Enable key check, calibration attempt, frame inspection, cable inspection, and weigh frame interference check were performed. Persistent error indicates likely scale system fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service. Labeled Out of Service. Performed power cycle and scale troubleshooting. No correctable external issue found. Escalated for scale P.C. board replacement and further repair evaluation.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err3 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err3 on the Hillrom VersaCare bed scale display when scale operation is affected by bed position, frame interference, cable damage, scale board failure, or load-cell faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the bed from patient use if accurate weight is clinically required. Do not rely on the bed scale while Err3 is active. Use another approved weighing method if patient weight is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the scale display shows Err3. Check when the error appears and confirm other bed functions operate normally so the issue is isolated to the scale system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Bed
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power, wait at least 10 seconds, reconnect AC power, and press Enable and Scale Control. If Err3 clears, perform function checks before returning the bed to service.

steps.title: Position the Bed Correctly
steps.instructions: Run the bed head section down, level the bed, and make sure the bed is not in an unusual position. Press Enable and Scale Control again and verify whether Err3 clears.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Interference or Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the frame and weigh frame for bent sections, foreign objects, loose hardware, impact damage, or interference between the weigh frame and non-weigh frame.

steps.title: Inspect Scale Cables and Load-Cell Wiring
steps.instructions: Inspect visible scale wiring and connectors for pinched cables, cut insulation, loose connectors, damaged load-cell wiring, fluid intrusion, or wiring pulled tight during bed movement.

steps.title: Recheck After External Corrections
steps.instructions: After correcting visible frame, interference, or cable issues, reconnect power, press Enable and Scale Control, operate the bed through normal movement, and confirm Err3 does not return.

steps.title: Inspect the Scale P.C. Board Indicators
steps.instructions: If no visible mechanical or wiring problem is found, inspect the scale P.C. board indicators. For European option beds, turn all DIP switches off before continuing and check P1 through P4 red LED status.

steps.title: Interpret P1–P4 Red LED Status
steps.instructions: If no P1–P4 red LEDs are on, suspect scale P.C. board failure. Replace the scale P.C. board according to facility procedure and perform required calibration afterward.

steps.title: Check Load-Cell Analog Error LEDs
steps.instructions: If one or more P1–P4 red LEDs are on, check whether all load-cell analog error LEDs are on. If not all are on, identify and replace the associated load cell, then calibrate the scale.

steps.title: Isolate Load Cells if All Analog Error LEDs Are On
steps.instructions: If all load-cell analog error LEDs are on, unplug one load cell at a time and watch for the analog error LED to turn off. Replace the load cell associated with the change, then calibrate the scale.

steps.title: Perform Calibration and Final Function Checks
steps.instructions: After replacing the scale P.C. board or any load cell, perform scale calibration and verify normal bed and scale operation before returning the bed to service.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed scale display shows Err3. Staff report scale will not operate normally and patient weight cannot be trusted.
CCR.Cause: Err3 confirmed on scale display. Bed power was cycled and bed was placed head-down and level. Frame, weigh frame, cable routing, and visible load-cell wiring inspected. Scale system fault remained, indicating likely scale P.C. board or load-cell fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service. Inspected for damaged frame, pinched cables, and weigh-frame interference. Checked scale P.C. board indicators and isolated suspected load-cell/scale board fault. Replaced faulty component as indicated, performed scale calibration, completed bed function checks, and verified Err3 cleared before return to service.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err4 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err4 on the Hillrom VersaCare bed scale display when a European scale configuration condition or scale electronics issue prevents reliable scale operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on the bed scale while Err4 is active. Remove the bed from patient use if accurate patient weight is required for care, medication dosing, fluid management, or clinical decision-making. Use another approved weighing method if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact error shown on the scale display is Err4. Identify whether it appears at power-up, when zeroing the scale, when weighing the patient, intermittently during use, or after service or configuration changes.

steps.title: Understand What Err4 Means
steps.instructions: Err4 is listed as Scale Error 4. Scale Error 4 and Scale Error 5 are identified as European Scale Only conditions. Treat this first as a scale configuration or DIP switch issue before assuming board failure.

steps.title: Check Basic External Conditions First
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is plugged into a known good outlet, inspect the power cord, confirm normal power behavior, and check for visible damage, fluid intrusion, or impact damage around scale-related areas.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Is Not Caused by Scale Use Conditions
steps.instructions: Make sure the bed is not touching walls, equipment, furniture, IV poles, linen carts, cords, tubing, or accessories that could affect weighing. Confirm the bed is on a stable floor surface and retest if safe.

steps.title: Check DIP Switch #4 Position
steps.instructions: Access the scale configuration area according to facility-approved service practice. Turn DIP switch #4 to the OFF position, power-cycle or reset the bed as needed, and recheck the scale display.

steps.title: If Err4 Clears, Perform Function Checks
steps.instructions: Verify scale display operation, zero function if applicable, weighing function, bed movement, siderail controls, brake, steer, lockout functions, and confirm no new errors appear before returning the bed to service.

steps.title: If Err4 Does Not Clear
steps.instructions: If DIP switch #4 is OFF and Err4 remains present, remove the bed from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for bench evaluation or repair. Replace the scale P.C. board if supported by your facility service process.

steps.title: Escalate If the Error Remains After Scale P.C. Board Replacement
steps.instructions: If Err4 remains after replacing the scale P.C. board, stop further troubleshooting and contact Hill-Rom Technical Support or approved repair support. Document the error, DIP switch position, board replacement, and final result.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed reported Err4 on the scale display. Scale function could not be trusted for patient weight readings.
CCR.Cause: Err4 identified as Scale Error 4. Checked for external weighing interference and verified scale configuration. DIP switch #4 required OFF position correction.
CCR.Resolution: Turned DIP switch #4 to OFF position and rechecked scale display. Err4 cleared. Performed bed function checks and verified scale operation before returning bed to service.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Error: Err5 Scale Display Error
description: Troubleshooting Err5 on the Hillrom VersaCare scale display when European scale configuration or scale board failure prevents normal scale operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on the bed scale while Err5 is active. Remove the bed from patient use if accurate bed weight is required for clinical care. Use another approved weighing method if patient weight is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the scale display shows Err5. Check when the error appears and confirm whether other bed functions operate normally.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is connected to a known-good AC outlet. Inspect the power cord and verify stable power before continuing scale troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Physical or Environmental Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect the scale display, side rails, base frame, load cell areas, cable pinch points, and under-bed components for damage, debris, fluid intrusion, or interference.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Context
steps.instructions: Check whether the bed recently had scale service, scale board replacement, configuration changes, DIP switch changes, or prior scale troubleshooting. Scale Error 4 and Scale Error 5 are identified as European Scale Only conditions.

steps.title: Check DIP Switch Configuration
steps.instructions: Turn DIP switch #4 to the OFF position and DIP switch #2 to the OFF position. Power cycle or reset the bed as required by facility procedure and recheck the scale display.

steps.title: Perform Function Checks if the Error Clears
steps.instructions: If Err5 clears, verify scale display operation, scale function, bed movement, control response, and that no new alarms or errors appear before returning the bed to service.

steps.title: Escalate if Err5 Does Not Clear
steps.instructions: If DIP switch #4 and DIP switch #2 are OFF and Err5 remains, remove the bed from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or bench evaluation. Replace the scale P.C. board if authorized.

steps.title: Contact Support if the Problem Remains
steps.instructions: If Err5 remains after scale P.C. board replacement, stop further basic troubleshooting and contact Hill-Rom Technical Support or qualified repair support.

CCR.Complaint: Hillrom VersaCare bed scale display showing Err5. Scale function unavailable or not reliable for patient weight measurement.
CCR.Cause: Err5 confirmed on scale display. Power and visible condition checked. Manual identifies Scale Error 4 and Scale Error 5 as European Scale Only conditions. DIP switch #4 and DIP switch #2 required OFF position verification.
CCR.Resolution: Set DIP switch #4 and DIP switch #2 to OFF. Err5 cleared. Performed bed and scale function checks. Bed passed checks and was returned to service.

title: Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher - Hydraulic Lift Will Not Raise or Lower Properly
description: Troubleshooting a stretcher that will not raise, lower, drifts, binds, or responds poorly to hydraulic lift controls.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use if height movement is unreliable, uneven, drifting, or difficult to control. Transfer the patient to another safe surface before troubleshooting whenever possible. Expected outcome: The stretcher is safe to inspect without risk of patient movement, fall, or uncontrolled height change.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Lift Problem
steps.instructions: Attempt to raise and lower the stretcher using the normal hydraulic pedals or controls. Observe whether the issue is no lift, slow lift, uneven lift, drifting down, failure to lower, or binding during travel. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before parts or hydraulic failure are assumed. If the stretcher operates normally during testing, document that the issue could not be duplicated and return it only after confirming safe function.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Obstruction Around the Base and Lift Frame
steps.instructions: Inspect underneath and around the stretcher for linens, cords, trash, equipment bags, foot pedals, transport straps, or other objects interfering with lift movement. Expected outcome: Nothing is physically blocking the lift mechanism or pedal travel. If an obstruction is found and removal restores normal lift function, stop and document the correction.

steps.title: Inspect the Hydraulic Pedals or Lift Controls
steps.instructions: Confirm the raise and lower pedals move freely and return to their normal position. Look for bent pedals, loose linkages, missing hardware, damage, or floor debris restricting movement. Expected outcome: Controls move smoothly and fully engage the lift/lower function. If a stuck or obstructed pedal is corrected and lift operation returns to normal, stop and document the repair.

steps.title: Confirm the Stretcher Is Not Overloaded
steps.instructions: Verify the stretcher is not being used beyond its rated capacity and that excess equipment, supplies, or accessories are not adding abnormal load. Expected outcome: The hydraulic system is being tested under appropriate load conditions. Why it matters: A weak or overloaded hydraulic system may raise slowly, fail to raise, or drift.

steps.title: Check Caster Position and Floor Contact
steps.instructions: Make sure all casters are properly contacting the floor and that the stretcher is on a level surface. Inspect for a caster or base issue that may make the frame appear uneven during height adjustment. Expected outcome: The stretcher is stable and not falsely presenting as a lift issue because of caster, floor, or frame position.

steps.title: Inspect for Visible Hydraulic Fluid Leakage
steps.instructions: Look around the hydraulic pump, lift cylinder area, base frame, and floor beneath the stretcher for oil, wet residue, or fresh dripping. Do not continue cycling the lift if an active leak is present. Expected outcome: No visible hydraulic fluid leakage is present. If fluid leakage is found, remove the stretcher from service and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

steps.title: Listen and Feel for Abnormal Pump Behavior
steps.instructions: Operate the lift pedal and note whether the pump feels unusually soft, fails to build pressure, requires excessive strokes, or makes abnormal clicking, grinding, or squeaking sounds. Expected outcome: The pump should build lift smoothly without excessive effort or abnormal noise. Why it matters: Weak pump action can point to a hydraulic, linkage, or lift component issue that requires repair.

steps.title: Check for Drifting After Raising
steps.instructions: Raise the stretcher to a safe test height and observe whether it slowly lowers without the lower control being activated. Keep hands and feet clear of moving parts. Expected outcome: The stretcher holds height without uncontrolled lowering. If the stretcher drifts down, remove it from service because the lift system may not safely support patient use.

steps.title: Check Lower Function Separately
steps.instructions: If the stretcher raises but will not lower, inspect the lower pedal or release control for restricted movement, bent components, or linkage that does not appear to engage. Do not force the pedal. Expected outcome: The lower control should release height smoothly and predictably. If the stretcher remains stuck raised or lowers unpredictably, remove it from service.

steps.title: Inspect for Frame Binding or Uneven Lift Movement
steps.instructions: Watch both sides of the litter/frame while raising and lowering. Look for twisting, hesitation, scraping, loose hardware, damaged linkage, or anything that causes one side to move differently than the other. Expected outcome: The lift moves evenly without binding, twisting, or abnormal resistance. If binding or frame damage is present, stop troubleshooting and escalate for repair.

steps.title: Perform a Final Functional Check Only If No Fault Is Found
steps.instructions: Cycle the stretcher through several raise and lower movements without a patient on it. Confirm smooth movement, proper stopping, no drift, no leaks, and stable frame position. Expected outcome: The stretcher raises, lowers, and holds height consistently. If the function is reliable, return the stretcher to service and document the checks performed.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher would not raise properly using the hydraulic lift pedal.
CCR.Cause: Found weak hydraulic lift response during testing with no external obstruction present and no safe reliable height hold.
CCR.Resolution: Removed stretcher from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation of the hydraulic lift system.

helpfulDetails: Whether the stretcher would not raise, would not lower, drifted down, or moved unevenly
Whether the issue occurred with or without patient load
Pedal movement and pedal return condition
Any unusual pump feel, noise, binding, or resistance
Caster and floor condition during testing
Whether lift frame movement was even on both sides
Alarm behavior, if any display or staff-reported warning was involved
Accessories swapped or removed during testing
Power behavior, if any powered accessory or control was involved
Environmental factors such as floor slope, debris, cords, linens, or transport conditions
Indicator lights, if any powered accessory or status indicator was involved
Final device status: returned to service or removed from service

title: Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher - Brake or Steer Pedal Does Not Engage, Release, or Hold
description: Troubleshooting brake or steer pedal problems, including failure to lock, release, hold position, or steer correctly.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use if the brake or steer function is unreliable. Transfer the patient to another stretcher if needed. Expected outcome: The stretcher is not used for transport or patient care until safe movement and braking are confirmed. Why it matters: A stretcher that rolls unexpectedly or will not steer properly can create a fall, collision, or transfer hazard.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Test the brake/steer pedal through its full range: brake, neutral, and steer positions. Confirm whether the issue is failure to engage, failure to release, drifting out of position, weak holding force, or poor steering control. Expected outcome: The exact failure mode is identified. If the pedal works normally during testing, document the condition and continue inspection.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Floor or Environmental Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect the floor surface for debris, uneven flooring, thresholds, wet areas, or wax buildup that may affect caster movement or brake holding. Expected outcome: The stretcher should brake and steer normally on a clean, level surface. If the issue only occurs in one area, the floor condition may be contributing.

steps.title: Inspect the Brake/Steer Pedal for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Look for a bent, cracked, loose, missing, or obstructed pedal. Check both sides if the stretcher has pedal access from multiple locations. Expected outcome: The pedal should move smoothly and return to the correct positions without binding. If the pedal is damaged, remove the stretcher from service.

steps.title: Check for Debris Around the Pedal and Linkage Area
steps.instructions: Look for linens, tape, packaging, mop strings, trash, or other debris caught around the pedal, brake bar, caster area, or visible linkage. Expected outcome: Removing external debris restores normal pedal movement. If resolved, retest brake and steer operation before returning to service.

steps.title: Inspect All Casters for Free Movement
steps.instructions: Roll the stretcher forward, backward, and sideways. Check for a caster that is locked, dragging, wobbling, noisy, or not swiveling correctly. Expected outcome: All casters should roll and swivel smoothly when the brake is released. A bad caster can feel like a brake or steer failure.

steps.title: Verify Brake Holding Function
steps.instructions: Place the pedal in the brake position on a level surface. Apply moderate pushing force from different directions. Expected outcome: The stretcher should remain stationary. If it rolls with the brake applied, remove it from service.

steps.title: Verify Brake Release Function
steps.instructions: Move the pedal to neutral/release. Push the stretcher in multiple directions. Expected outcome: The stretcher should roll freely without dragging. If a brake remains partially engaged, the brake mechanism or caster assembly may need repair.

steps.title: Verify Steer Function
steps.instructions: Place the pedal in the steer position and push the stretcher forward. Confirm whether the steering caster or fifth wheel function engages and tracks correctly. Expected outcome: The stretcher should track straighter and feel controlled during transport. If it wanders, binds, or does not engage steer mode, continue inspection.

steps.title: Check for Bent Frame Components or Impact Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the lower frame, caster mounts, brake bar area, and pedal side for signs of collision damage. Look for uneven caster contact or frame distortion. Expected outcome: No visible damage should interfere with brake or steer operation. If impact damage is present, remove the stretcher from service.

steps.title: Check for Missing or Loose External Hardware
steps.instructions: Look for loose bolts, missing fasteners, detached pedal covers, damaged linkage guards, or loose caster mounting hardware. Do not perform deep disassembly in the care area. Expected outcome: All visible hardware should be secure. If hardware is missing or looseness affects brake/steer safety, remove from service.

steps.title: Perform a Final Operational Check
steps.instructions: Test brake, neutral, and steer positions several times. Roll the stretcher, stop it, lock it, release it, and steer it under controlled conditions. Expected outcome: Brake holds, release is complete, steer engages correctly, and no abnormal noise or binding is present. If confirmed, return the stretcher to service.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Hillrom P8000 stretcher brake pedal would not hold and the stretcher rolled after the brake was applied.
CCR.Cause: Found brake/steer pedal movement abnormal and brake holding force unreliable after checking floor condition, caster movement, and visible pedal obstruction.
CCR.Resolution: Removed stretcher from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation of the brake/steer mechanism and caster assemblies.

helpfulDetails: Brake pedal position tested: brake, neutral, and steer
Whether the brake failed to engage, release, or hold
Casters checked for rolling, swiveling, wobble, or dragging
Any visible pedal, linkage, frame, or caster damage
Any abnormal noise, binding, or grinding
Whether debris was found around the pedal or casters
Whether the stretcher passed or failed final brake/steer testing
Final device status: returned to service or removed from service

title: Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher - Side rail will not latch, release, or stays loose
description: Troubleshooting a P8000 side rail that will not lock, release, or remains loose during use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use if the side rail cannot be trusted to stay locked. Transfer the patient to another stretcher or bed if needed. Expected outcome: The patient is protected from fall risk before troubleshooting begins. If this resolves the immediate safety concern, continue only when the stretcher is empty and safe to inspect.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Side Rail Issue
steps.instructions: Raise and lower the affected side rail several times. Confirm whether the issue is failure to latch, failure to release, excessive looseness, binding, or incomplete movement. Expected outcome: The exact failure mode is identified before parts are adjusted or replaced.

steps.title: Compare With the Opposite Side Rail
steps.instructions: Operate the matching side rail on the other side of the stretcher and compare latch feel, release handle movement, rail travel, and looseness. Expected outcome: A normal reference point helps confirm whether the affected rail is abnormal.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the side rail, latch hook, release handle, pivot points, mounting hardware, plastic covers, and surrounding frame area for cracks, bends, missing parts, or impact damage. Expected outcome: Any visible damage is identified. If the rail or latch hardware is cracked, bent, or structurally damaged, stop and remove the stretcher from service.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstruction Around the Latch and Release Mechanism
steps.instructions: Look for linens, tape, patient belongings, debris, broken plastic, or dried contamination interfering with the rail movement or latch engagement. Expected outcome: The latch and release path are clear. If debris or obstruction is found, remove it according to facility cleaning policy and retest the rail. If the rail now latches and releases normally, return to service after confirming safe operation.

steps.title: Check That the Side Rail Is Being Raised Fully Into the Latch Position
steps.instructions: Raise the rail with controlled upward motion until it reaches the locking point. Listen and feel for a positive latch engagement. Expected outcome: The rail should lock firmly without needing excessive force. If the rail only latches when forced, treat it as unreliable and continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Release Handle Movement
steps.instructions: Operate the release handle or release control and verify it moves freely, returns properly, and is not sticking halfway. Expected outcome: The release mechanism should move smoothly and return to its resting position. If the release handle sticks or does not return, remove the stretcher from service for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect Mounting Hardware Externally
steps.instructions: Check for loose, missing, or visibly shifted fasteners at the side rail mounts and hinge/pivot areas. Do not perform deep disassembly during floor troubleshooting. Expected outcome: The rail should not move excessively at the mounting points. If hardware is loose, missing, or the rail assembly shifts under light pressure, remove from service for repair.

steps.title: Check for Excessive Side-to-Side or Vertical Play
steps.instructions: With the rail latched, apply gentle movement to the rail to check for looseness. Do not use excessive force. Expected outcome: The side rail should remain secure with only normal minor movement. If the rail stays loose while latched, remove the stretcher from service because the latch or mounting assembly may not be safe.

steps.title: Check Stretcher Frame Alignment Near the Affected Rail
steps.instructions: Inspect the surrounding litter frame and rail mounting area for signs of impact, bent brackets, or uneven spacing compared with the opposite side. Expected outcome: The rail should align correctly with the latch receiver. If the rail does not align with the latch, the issue is likely mechanical damage or misalignment requiring bench repair.

steps.title: Clean Accessible Latch Areas if Contamination Is Suspected
steps.instructions: Use approved facility cleaning methods to remove visible dirt or sticky residue from accessible external latch and handle areas. Avoid spraying fluids directly into mechanisms. Expected outcome: The mechanism operates more freely after cleaning. If cleaning restores normal latch and release function, confirm repeated operation before returning the stretcher to service.

steps.title: Perform a Final Operational Check
steps.instructions: Raise, latch, release, and lower the side rail multiple times. Confirm it locks positively, releases only when intended, and does not remain loose. Expected outcome: The side rail operates reliably and consistently. If the rail passes repeated testing, document the work and return the stretcher to service.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Hillrom P8000 side rail would not latch securely and felt loose when raised.
CCR.Cause: Found affected side rail latch area clear of obstruction, but rail remained loose while latched, indicating likely latch or mounting hardware wear.
CCR.Resolution: Removed stretcher from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench repair evaluation of the side rail latch and mounting assembly.

helpfulDetails: Whether the rail would not latch, would not release, or stayed loose
Whether the opposite side rail operated normally
Any visible damage to rail, latch, handle, or frame
Whether mounting hardware appeared loose or missing
Any alarm behavior if the stretcher has related indicators or safety alerts
Any accessories swapped or removed during inspection
Any power behavior if powered accessories or indicators are involved
Environmental factors such as impact damage, transport use, linens, fluids, tape, or contamination
Indicator lights or visible status indicators if present

title: Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher - Fowler/backrest, Trendelenburg, or knee section movement failure
description: Troubleshooting stretcher section movement problems caused by locks, release handles, obstructions, damaged linkages, hydraulics, or mechanical binding.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use if the Fowler, Trendelenburg, or knee section will not move smoothly, will not hold position, drops unexpectedly, or feels unstable. Expected outcome: The patient is moved to another safe stretcher or surface before troubleshooting continues. Why it matters: Section movement failures can create fall, pinch, positioning, or injury risk.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Movement Problem
steps.instructions: Identify which section is affected: Fowler/backrest, Trendelenburg/reverse Trendelenburg, knee section, or multiple sections. Operate the control or release mechanism slowly while observing the motion. Expected outcome: The failure is verified and narrowed to one section or a larger frame/control issue. If the section moves normally during testing, document the finding and return to service only if the stretcher passes a full functional check.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions Around the Litter Frame
steps.instructions: Inspect under and around the moving section for linens, straps, patient belongings, cables, oxygen tubing, IV tubing, debris, or accessories blocking travel. Expected outcome: The section moves freely once the obstruction is removed. If this resolves the issue, stop and document the obstruction and final function test.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress Position and Accessory Interference
steps.instructions: Confirm the mattress is seated correctly and not wedged into the hinge area. Check attached accessories such as IV poles, side rails, trays, brackets, or positioning devices that may interfere with movement. Expected outcome: The moving section has clear travel and is not mechanically blocked. If repositioning the mattress or accessory restores normal movement, stop and document the correction.

steps.title: Verify Release Handle or Control Operation
steps.instructions: Operate the Fowler, Trendelenburg, or knee section release/control and confirm it returns normally after use. Look for a loose, bent, broken, sticky, or disconnected handle. Expected outcome: The handle moves smoothly and appears to engage the release mechanism. If the handle is damaged or does not return properly, remove the stretcher from service for repair.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Binding or Uneven Movement
steps.instructions: Slowly raise and lower the affected section while watching both sides of the frame. Look for twisting, uneven lift, binding, grinding, popping, or a section that catches during travel. Expected outcome: Both sides move evenly without abnormal resistance or noise. If binding or uneven motion is present, do not force the section. Remove the stretcher from service.

steps.title: Inspect Visible Pivot Points, Linkages, and Mounting Hardware
steps.instructions: Without deep disassembly, visually inspect accessible hinges, pivot points, support arms, brackets, pins, and fasteners for obvious looseness, damage, missing hardware, or misalignment. Expected outcome: No visible structural damage or missing hardware is found. If hardware is missing, bent, cracked, or visibly damaged, label the stretcher Out of Service and send for repair.

steps.title: Check Whether the Section Holds Position
steps.instructions: Raise the affected section to a safe test position and verify it remains stable without drifting down. Do not place hands or body parts under a raised section. Expected outcome: The section holds position without drifting, slipping, or dropping. If the section drifts, drops, or will not support position, remove the stretcher from service immediately.

steps.title: Inspect for Hydraulic or Gas Spring Concerns
steps.instructions: For sections assisted by hydraulic or gas spring mechanisms, look for oil leakage, weak lift assist, sudden drop, poor resistance, damaged cylinders, or abnormal movement. Expected outcome: No leak, loss of support, or uncontrolled movement is observed. If leakage, weak support, or uncontrolled movement is found, the issue is likely internal/mechanical and requires repair.

steps.title: Perform a Full Stretcher Function Check
steps.instructions: After any correction, test Fowler/backrest movement, Trendelenburg/reverse Trendelenburg movement, knee section movement, brake/steer function, caster movement, side rails, and overall frame stability. Expected outcome: All functions operate smoothly and safely before the stretcher is returned to service. If any function remains abnormal, keep the stretcher out of service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Hillrom P8000 Fowler section would not raise and felt stuck during positioning.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found the Fowler section movement was blocked by a linen bundle caught near the backrest hinge area.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the obstruction, verified smooth Fowler, Trendelenburg, and knee section movement, and returned the stretcher to service after functional testing.

helpfulDetails: Which section failed: Fowler/backrest, Trendelenburg, reverse Trendelenburg, or knee section
Whether the issue was intermittent or constant
Whether the section would not raise, would not lower, drifted, dropped, or bound during movement
Linens, straps, tubing, or accessories checked for obstruction
Visible hinge, linkage, bracket, pin, or fastener damage
Any unusual noise, binding, grinding, or uneven movement
Evidence of fluid leak, weak support, or sudden drop
Power behavior, if any powered accessory or connected component was involved
Environmental factors such as wet floors, debris, tight rooms, or accessory crowding around the stretcher
Indicator lights or displayed messages, if equipped
Final device status: returned to service or removed from service

title: Hillrom P8000 Procedural Stretcher - Casters, fifth wheel, litter frame, or mattress support damaged/noisy
description: Troubleshooting noisy, damaged, loose, binding, or unstable caster, fifth wheel, litter frame, or mattress support concerns on a procedural stretcher.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from patient use before troubleshooting if there is any concern with stability, rolling control, frame damage, or mattress support integrity. Expected outcome: The patient is transferred to another safe stretcher or bed before inspection continues. If this resolves the immediate safety risk, stop and document the action.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Ask staff what was noticed: squeaking, grinding, wobbling, hard steering, uneven rolling, frame flexing, mattress sagging, or visible damage. Roll the stretcher in a clear area and listen for the reported noise. Expected outcome: The issue is confirmed and narrowed to casters, fifth wheel, frame, or mattress support area.

steps.title: Perform a Visual Safety Inspection
steps.instructions: Inspect the stretcher from all sides. Look for bent metal, cracked welds, loose hardware, missing fasteners, damaged covers, broken supports, or signs that the stretcher struck a wall, elevator, doorway, or equipment. Expected outcome: Obvious damage is identified before functional testing continues. If structural damage is found, remove from service and stop.

steps.title: Check Each Caster for Damage or Debris
steps.instructions: Inspect all caster wheels for flat spots, cracks, missing chunks, embedded debris, wrapped tape, hair, string, or buildup around the axle. Rotate each wheel by hand if safe. Expected outcome: Each caster rotates smoothly without grinding, rubbing, or excessive side play. If debris is removed and the caster rolls normally, return to service only after a full function check.

steps.title: Check Caster Swivel Movement
steps.instructions: With the stretcher unloaded, move it forward, backward, and side to side. Watch whether each caster swivels freely or binds. Expected outcome: Casters pivot smoothly without hopping, dragging, or locking unexpectedly. If a caster binds, wobbles, or has excessive play, remove from service for caster repair or replacement.

steps.title: Check Brake and Steer Interaction
steps.instructions: Cycle the brake/steer pedals through brake, neutral, and steer positions. Confirm the reported caster or fifth wheel issue is not actually caused by a partially engaged brake or steer mechanism. Expected outcome: The stretcher rolls freely in neutral, locks in brake, and tracks properly in steer. If brake/steer adjustment or linkage concern is suspected, remove from service for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect the Fifth Wheel Area
steps.instructions: Look underneath the center area of the stretcher for a damaged fifth wheel, loose hardware, debris, worn tread, dragging components, or anything contacting the floor incorrectly. Expected outcome: The fifth wheel engages and disengages cleanly without dragging, clunking, scraping, or causing unstable tracking. If the fifth wheel is damaged, loose, or not tracking correctly, remove from service.

steps.title: Check for Unusual Noise While Rolling
steps.instructions: Roll the stretcher slowly over a smooth floor, then over a doorway threshold if safe. Listen for squeaking, clicking, grinding, clunking, or rattling. Expected outcome: Noise is repeatable and traceable to a caster, fifth wheel, frame area, side rail area, or mattress support area. If the noise is caused by loose external hardware that can be safely secured, correct it and retest.

steps.title: Inspect the Litter Frame
steps.instructions: Raise and lower the litter if the stretcher is equipped for height adjustment. Watch for twisting, uneven movement, rubbing, loose frame components, cracked welds, or abnormal flexing. Expected outcome: The litter frame remains stable and level without excessive movement or noise. If the frame appears bent, cracked, unstable, or misaligned, remove from service immediately.

steps.title: Inspect the Mattress Support Surface
steps.instructions: Remove or lift the mattress if needed and inspect the deck/support surface. Look for broken panels, bent supports, sharp edges, loose screws, missing hardware, sagging sections, or areas that could damage the mattress or create pressure points. Expected outcome: The mattress support is secure, smooth, and able to support the patient safely. If the mattress support is damaged or unstable, remove from service.

steps.title: Inspect the Mattress Condition
steps.instructions: Check the mattress for tears, bottom-side damage, compression, fluid intrusion, or damage caused by the support surface underneath. Expected outcome: The mattress is intact and properly supported. If the mattress is damaged or contaminated, replace or route according to facility policy.

steps.title: Check Accessory Mounts and Attachments
steps.instructions: Inspect IV pole sockets, oxygen tank holders, side rail mounts, push handles, and any attached accessories near the noisy or damaged area. Loose accessories can sound like frame or caster problems. Expected outcome: Accessories are secure and not creating false noise or instability. If an accessory is loose or damaged, correct or remove it before returning the stretcher to service.

steps.title: Perform a Final Function and Safety Check
steps.instructions: After any simple correction, roll the stretcher, steer it, brake it, inspect the frame, and confirm the mattress support is secure. Expected outcome: The stretcher rolls smoothly, tracks correctly, brakes securely, and has no unsafe structural or support concerns. If the issue is resolved, document the work and return the stretcher to service.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Hillrom P8000 procedural stretcher made a grinding noise while rolling and appeared difficult to steer during transport.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found debris wrapped around one caster and excessive play/noise from the fifth wheel area during steer testing.
CCR.Resolution: Removed debris from caster, verified continued fifth wheel noise, labeled stretcher Out of Service, and routed for bench repair evaluation.
